title: KitchenAid Burr Coffee Grinder - KCG8433 - Black Matte, 10 Oz
description:
About this item
Our grinder automatically produces the ideal amount and consistency of grounds for every drink selection with Smart Dosing Technology.
Getting consistent grind size means delicious brewing every time. Achieve consistent grind sizes for all brew methods from French Press to Espresso with the KitchenAid Burr Coffee Grinder.
Discover your perfect grind, for every kind of brew. With 70 precise settings for a wide variety of grind sizes - from coarse for Cold Brew and French Press, to fine for Espresso and espresso drinks.
Commercial-grade, stainless steel conical burrs provide superior grinding precision, producing uniformly-sized grounds for optimal flavor extraction.
No need to flip your machine upside down—simply twist and lift the removable bean hopper to empty, exchange or refill with fresh beans between brews.
reviews_5s:
I watched a bunch of grinder reviews before buying this. Most of them don't include this product, as it's lesser known and doesn't seem to be considered a serious contender among coffee aficionados. Common wisdom seems to be to go with the Baratza Encore at this price point, or maybe something from Breville, Felllow, or Varia. I'm glad I didn't.

The big appeals for me here were looks, quiet operation, and automatic dosing. It's pretty quiet, quiet enough that it doesn't seem to bother my cat sleeping nearby in the morning. You can dial in a specific dose via grind time, and it will save it and gets it consistent enough that I don't have to weigh beans anymore, I just press the button and use it, and it's one in about 9 seconds. Plus, it looks pretty nice and has a small counter footprint.

Visually, the grind quality appears to be a bit more consistent than my $60 Timemore hand grinder is, and it's FAR faster.

The only real downside I can recall people mentioning was grind quality, but to me the grind quality is great, and even if a Baratza gives a more consistent particle size, ask yourself: are you really going to be able to tell the difference in coffee flavor between two different electrical conical burr grinders? Honestly? In a blind test, you think you could tell? Maybe so, but I certainly can't. This is a great pick.Your journey for a quiet and reliable grinder is over - learn from my mistakes! This one now sits next to my Moccamaster and completes my mornings (and mid mornings and afternoons and sometimes right after dinner...).

Previously had a blade grinder in my poor and single days - super noisy, but did an ok job.

Next grinder, just wanted something better but not crazy expensive - it was one of these fake company burr grinders (if you notice, there are like 20 different companies all selling the same grinder, each with a different weird name on it). It was quieter than the blade grinder, worked decently, but I made the mistake of trying to clean it one day and didn't notice a very important, and apparently not replaceable part, fall out never to be seen, rendering that mediocre grinder inoperable.

My second mistake was thinking that I'd have a similar ok experience with yet another no-name burr grinder - this one was a complete disaster - grinder would get stuck trying to grind beans every time, so dosing was completely random, and it made a mess. Sent it back right away.

Finally - buy nice or buy twice - here I was looking to be done with buying grinders for a while. Read reviews, and everyone seemed to recommend obscenely expensive grinders, with a few exceptions that always had some deal-breaker (noisy, or made a mess, or inconsistent, etc).

Saw this Kitchenaid out of the corner of my eye, and remembered that our stand mixer has been a staple in multiple kitchens for like 30yrs now. So I decided to take a leap of faith after reading mostly positive reviews.

Man, this thing it great! SUPER QUIET - almost pleasant to listen to - like a healthy-sounding V8 instead of a 4-banger with a CVT. The method to select grind size and dosing is great and simple. One button to go first thing in the morning with no fuss, no mess, and then great results - a great cup of coffee every time. Can't complain at all... Being from MI originally, I love that this was perhaps designed locally, so this burr grinder gets a double-thumbs up from this guy...I've had a few coffee grinders in my life, most far more bulkier and 10x louder than this one. This one is relatively compact, very quiet, and for drip/pourover coffee gives a great grind.

I don't do espresso, so I won't comment on how appropriate this grinder might be for that. For straight drip or pourover however, it gives a very consistent grind with minimal fines and course. The time based doses are easy to set, and consistent.

I'm not so focused on my coffee that I feel the need to weigh out each dose when making coffee. I enjoy good coffee, I prefer fresh ground beans, but I also value convenience. This grinder brings all of that to one simple appliance. Possibly the best feature of this, and I don't know how they do it, is there is next to no static.

If you enjoy fresh ground coffee, but don't want to weigh each time, and want a consistent well ground does, I urge you to try this grinder. No complaints.I only got this 3 days ago so it's probably too soon for a review, however, I'm really impressed with it. It works perfect for a drip coffee maker, very quiet, 100% mess free, and looks really nice on the counter. It's not as big and overwhelming as it looks in some of the pictures. It's also really simple to figure out. You can simply look at it and understand how to use it.We LOVE this grinder; it brings a smile every morning!

We do pour-over and debated whether we really needed to upgrade our older, entry-level burr grinder. Despite our initial questioning, we have been extremely happy with this KitchenAid Burr Coffee Grinder.

▪︎ Quiet
▪︎ Very consistent grind size. We're actually able to do an espresso level grind and get very full bodied pour over coffee that is not bitter.
▪︎ Great design for the bean hopper. This is very convenient for adding / changing beans and cleaning.
▪︎ We've had essentially no problems with static. This is great, because our old burr grinder is a static nightmare.
▪︎ Many options to dial in grind size, grind time, no. of cups. We used a scale in the beginning to dial in on settings for our morning coffees, but no longer need the scale as the KitchenAid grinder has produced very consistent results.
▪︎ KitchenAid quality - we've had great experience with various KitchenAid products since the 'Hobart' days and as a result, have high expectations that we'll get many years of great service from this burr grinder.

Wish we had bought this earlier!This replaced a different brand of burr grinder that was touted as virtually indestructible. After a little searching of reviews I chose this one and so far it lives up to what folks had said. Grinders are noisy but this one is substantially more quiet than the one it replaced. It’s easy to use and easy to customize the grind size and time of grind to get exactly how much you want. But it was spot on first grind without adjusting anything. It is taller than the old one but still fit under my cabinets. I hope it lasts…Grinds the correct amount of coffee beans for a single or double shot. The amount can be adjusted up and down or you can just grind a huge batch to store in the airtight bottom container. The grind can be adjusted to fine tune how fine or course you want your coffee. Also will grind for lattes etc. This is a high quality machine which should last for years of daily use. I am super happy with the consistent results with this machine. I am pairing it with an Empire Red KitchenAid espresso maker. The work well together and look great on my kitchen counter. IMHO, these machines are worth every penny.We have been using this grinder for almost a month and from the first grind out of the box we knew that we had a quality product. It is much quieter than we expected and the anti- static grind bin is a very big win for us. The grind quality is great and it is very easy to disassemble for cleaning. We use a press pot, espresso machine and a drip pot and the grind quality, size and consistency for each setting is exactly what we were looking for. Like most people, we have purchased numerous small appliances and electronics over the years, often from online sources without first seeing the products in person. As many have discovered, we live in an age of disposable goods and many of these devices aren't built to last past the warranty dates. I can't speak for the longevity of this grinder yet, but I can easily describe this unit as solid, substantial and accurately described. This is a rare find in today's era which actually delivers on its promises and looks to be able to do so for years to come. Excepting maybe the most refined tasters among us, this grinder will satisfy and exceed the expectations of most people.

It also looks nice set out on a countertop full time so it isn't necessary to store away when it isn't being used.I bought this to replace my cheap blade grinder to see if I could get better quality ground coffee. I didn't like buying ground coffee and losing out on the freshness factor. Also grinding coffee at the store always felt subject to getting mixed grounds as everyone was using it, and I had no idea when or if the store even cleaned out their grinder. Everyone said burr grinders were better than blade and I would agree now that I have one. The consistency of the ground is much better than a blade grinder. Having the hopper on top(while exposed to light and air)gives up on storing your coffee in airtight containers and out of light but since I make coffee every morning, its a small sacrifice I am willing to make. Others can still do this and then just measure out what you need in beans too, so you have options. Even though I am using the hopper I can still measure out my 32 grams of coffee based on programmable grind time. Different coffees require different grind times and being able to program a set grind time is great. Finding the adjustable dial in the middle of the grinder took a moment as it is not labeled well, but once discovered, made finding my consistent grind times easy. The container that holds the grinds is amply sized as well but the grinder's vertical build keeps the footprint on the counter small which is also great. The maker also says the container is designed to minimise the coffee grounds sticking to the container and I have found this to be true but only 99% which is better than my old blade grinder. Others have reported this feature to not really be the case, but what I have found is just wiping the small amount of excess grounds out of the container when done and then putting it back has maintained the (anti-static coating?) containers ability to not hold onto the grounds. Other users may have washed their container with soap and water which may have removed any ability for grounds to not stick to the container but I have no way of proving this and I don't intend to use soap and water on mine. I started with just paper towels, but I have since moved on to using a cloth I got from the eye doctor for my glasses. I just wipe down the inside of the container, shake out the cloth, and put it back in a plastic baggy. After weeks of use, my grounds still slide right out of the container and into my french press. As for cleaning, This is machine is very easy to take apart, the grinder comes right out, easy to wash and dry and put back in. But you will notice after taking it out, that excess coffee grounds have built up a little. The included brush is good for getting some of it out, but not great. This goes back to my store grinder comment, is the store really cleaning out their grinders? Probably not. This is where YMMV. I have taken to using the provided brush and a can of compressed air to get the excess grounds out. You of course have to do this over your sink carefully or outside which requires unplugging the machine unless you want to get coffee grounds everywhere. I do this about every two weeks once I run out of beans. Do you have to do this? No. Some may just remove the grinder, wash it, brush the excess grinds out and then call it a day. Again it's up to you, but again you have options so YMMV as I said. Overall, for the price versus other burr grinders I have seen, this one is well worth the purchase.the burr grinder it replaced. This one grinds a more consistent size and perhaps more importantly is so much quieter than the burr grinder (different brand) it replaced. This one feels like a slightly loud conversation versus someone yelling. Yes there are quieter burr grinders but those cost ALOT more. My previous Grinder was a cuisinart. Nothing fancy. It was loud, in fluctuating grind volume and inconsistent size of grind (I mainly French press but once in a while like espresso). This Kitchenaid is such an improvement. Accurate volume of doses, quiet, and consistent grind size. Couldn’t be happier.
reviews_1s:
This is a great coffee grinder and I am very pleased with it. However, it is way more sophisticated than it needs to be. I did not need all these automatic features, but I have found a work-around that allows me to use it the way I prefer. It is designed for someone who wants to fill up the hopper with beans and then dial in how many "cups" of coffee you want to grind. The problem is that nowhere in any of the instructions is a "cup" defined. Is it 5-ounces? 6-ounces? 8-ounces? 12-ounces? 16-ounces? It makes a difference, after all, and you need to know, but it's an undefined quantity. It assumes that you will be satisfied with the amount of coffee that the machine puts out based on its undefined "cup." So, let me explain my process. I only put the amount of beans required for my recipe into the hopper. I then dial in the info (which was determined by trial and error) that will make the grinder work until all the beans are ground and then shut off. It would have been so much simpler if a simple switch had been provided for me to turn it on to start and then off when finished.

Now, this is what I really like about this grinder, in no particular order of importance. It is by far the quietest electric burr grinder that I have ever owned, and I have had many, including an older KitchenAid which is the loudest one ever. So, the noise level was a pleasant surprise. The bean hopper and the grounds container are both plastic. Had I known this I would never have bought it because of bad experience I have had with static electricity in the grounds catcher. The plastic grounds catchers in other grinders I have had were just nasty. The grounds had a tendency to go everywhere besides into the catcher and the grounds that went into the catcher then did not want to come out. My cabinet top was always covered with stray coffee grounds. But, guess what? So far, in the few times that I have used this grinder there has been no static problem whatsoever. I am extremely pleased with that. This grinder has 70 grind levels and every level I have placed it on has produced a consistent grind with practically no silt. This is very important to producing a great cup of joe and probably contributes to the lack of static, as well.

Yes, it looks good sitting on your cabinet, if that is important to you. And it is definitely high tech, if you are into that. I would have preferred a simple on-off switch.

Update: It worked just fine for about three weeks. I was very happy with it. However, a few days ago it stopped grinding before it was finished. It displayed "Error 2." The troubleshooting guide said the solution was to clean the grinding mechanism and explained how to do it. I have cleaned it spotlessly for well over a dozen times to no avail. It refuses to operate properly. After every cleaning it will grind for three or four seconds and then cut off. So, the cleaning does absolutely no good. Apparently, its motor is extremely weak and clogs if it makes contact with a random coffee bean. It was a great coffee grinder for three weeks. Now it is a piece of trash that needs to be discarded. What a disappointment.I bought it in February 2024. Today is 1/12/2025, and it no longer works correctly. I used to make a cup of coffee in the morning and sometimes in the afternoon until one day, I heard a noise and it started to take double the time to grind the same amount of coffee, since then is getting worse and worse. I also have noticed that it has to be cleaned more frequently as time goes by. I think is because the expeller is plastic and the coffee had been working as sandpaper on the plastic. For the money I paid for it, I think I should get a better product. I own many KitchenAid electrodomestic in my house but this one has disappointed me so far.Spent 45 minutes on the phone with Kitchenaid (Whirlpool), to be told that they would send me a refurbished replacement when mine stopped working in month 13 of use.

Once I had already mailed in mine, they canceled the replacement order without notifying me and now I need to call back again to get it straightened out.

Save yourself the headache and don’t buy this grinder. Not worth it.This thing is junk. I have had two now and both have failed. The first one I received initially worked flawlessly. It was quiet and had a nice grind. After about two weeks it began to fail. I called Kitchen Aid and they replaced the faulty unit within a week or so of my call to customer service. They informed me they would send a shipping label with the replacement grinder, so I could return the original faulty one. They never sent the shipping label. The replacement grinder did work slightly longer than the original but ultimately failed in the same fashion. I called Kitchen Aid’s customer service to get another replacement with intentions of giving it one more chance. I was informed I had to send the original grinder back, which would have already been done, had they sent the label as they indicated they would. Due to this, I was quoted an estimated replacement time of 6 weeks to account for them sending the new label, return shipping time, processing it in, and shipping the new one. Upon expressing displeasure, I was offered a buy back on the faulty grinder. The process would be similar. They would send a label to return the original one and I would return it. Once they received it, I could call back in about six weeks and wait through the endless hold times to formally request my money back. I was told once the request was made it would take about 8 weeks for them to issue the refund. It’s obvious the process is designed to delay action so you get frustrated and give up. Based on my experience, I will not likely buy anything else made by Kitchen Aid.This grinder worked for less than 2 months! The return window closed after a month so now we are out $180 with a faulty product. We will contact KitchenAid to try to work with them but we would recommend staying away from this grinder. All the sudden it just stopped grinding the right amount of coffee, it was great and then just stopped working correctly. Lots of bells and whistles but ultimately junk!I purchased a brand new one but got a used one for the price of a brand new product. The advertised product is a brand new product but that is false. Buyers be aware.This is the second one I've owned. First one was great for 3 months then would grind half the amount it used to so I increased the grind time until a 10 months later just wasn't worth it. Bought another, cleaned the grinder chamber every month, fully disassembling the grinder head and everything. This morning made a strange noise half way through the grind and wouldn't grind anymore. Cleaned it out, it worked without beans and then added them and same problem. Never again. Kitchen Aid grinder is trashThis is a great grinder while it works and worth the money quite! Unfortunately the love story came to an end after four months of grinding coffee for two. Started to make a little different sounds and then quit grinding and sounded like the motor was running but not grinding. Four months for $180 not the same as quality as my other Kitchen Aid product!Even on the coarsest setting this grinder still grinds fine. That many grind settings and none of them grind coarse? I have a hand grinder that can manage this, and it costs nowhere near what this machine does. I drink french press or pour over, so this grinder is useless to me. Ignore the reviews unless you’re an espresso drinker. That’s all this is good for.The grinder looks good and does a decent job grinding, although the largest size is a bit fine. When opening the container the coffee instantly goes everywhere from the static. After a year of trying different solutions I am going back to my trusty hand powered grinder.
